title: phoenicaulis cheiranthoides subsp. glabra
intro: Phoenicaulis cheiranthoides subsp. glabra, also known as Desert Larkspur or Glabrous Phoenicaulis, is a rare, flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Phoenicaulis cheiranthoides subsp. glabra is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
